_comment: EKUBO V3 MEVCaptureRouter (EkuboProtocol/evm-contracts v3.1.1 Router.sol; ETH mainnet
0xd26f20001a72a18C002b00e6710000d68700ce00) — the E0-frozen recipe surface. Signatures
line-verified against the pinned v3.1.1 sources AND selector-checked against the DEPLOYED
runtime 2026-07-04: quote((address,address,bytes32),bool,int128,uint96,uint256) = 0x3bc52842
(re-probed live: +1000e18 USDe -> delta0 +1000e18 / delta1 -998.69e6);
swap((address,address,bytes32),bool,int128,uint96,uint256,int256,address) = 0xf196187f
(fork-EXECUTED: pulled exactly the consumed input via transferFrom, paid the recipient the
quoted out to the wei, allowance residue 0, threshold == quote did not trip);
swapAllowPartialFill((address,address,bytes32),bool,int128,uint96,uint256,address) =
0x4adbe0cc (present, UNUSED — E0 pinned exec Option A: the partial-fill overloads hardcode
type(int256).min and DISABLE the slippage check). Underlying value types: SqrtRatio = uint96,
PoolConfig = bytes32, PoolBalanceUpdate = PoolState = bytes32 (declared uint256 below —
same raw word, and uint256 keeps the engine's return decode from intN-masking the packed
int128 lanes). quote is DECLARED NONPAYABLE so the compiler emits a PLAIN CALL: the lock
protocol TSTOREs, so a STATICCALL context breaks it (the Fluid/Metric quoter class); the
inner lock unwinds via the internal QuoteReturnValue revert, so the CALL is state-neutral
and probe-then-decode (two sequential CALLs) is safe. Revert classes (E0): uninitialized
pool -> PoolNotInitialized() 0x486aa307; oversize exact-in -> GRACEFUL partial fill (the
consumed in-delta < specified); full-fill swap -> PartialSwapsDisallowed() when consumed !=
amount, SlippageCheckFailed(int256,int256) below threshold; sqrtRatioLimit 0 -> the router
substitutes the direction-correct MIN/MAX bound (never SqrtRatioLimitWrongDirection).
